title: Main Control Board Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: The F1 E1 error code on an Amana dryer indicates the main electronic control board has experienced an internal communication or memory error. The board is unable to properly execute its programming. This may be resolved by a power reset but often requires board replacement.

causes.cause: Temporary electrical glitch or power surge
Loose wiring connection to the control board
Faulty power supply to the dryer

symptoms: Dryer does not respond to controls

fixSteps.title: Power reset the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer from the outlet or turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Plug it back in and test.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the outlet provides proper voltage (240V for electric, 120V for gas). Test with a multimeter at the outlet.

fixSteps.title: Inspect control board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, remove the top panel to access the control board. Check all wire harness connections for loose or corroded pins. Reseat all connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the main control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, order the correct replacement control board for your model. Disconnect all harness connectors, remove mounting screws, and install the new board.

whenToCallPro: If the power reset does not resolve the error, professional diagnosis is recommended before purchasing an expensive control board.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer F1E1 control board error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F1E1 on my Amana dryer?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can cause temporary control board errors. Try a power reset first. Consider using a surge protector.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the dryer control board?
faq.a: If the dryer is less than 8 years old, replacing the control board is usually more cost-effective than buying a new dryer.

faq.q: How do I know if my control board is bad?
faq.a: If a power reset does not clear the F1E1 code and all wiring connections are secure, the control board likely needs replacement.

title: User Interface Communication Error
shortDescription: The control panel cannot communicate with the main control board.
fullDescription: The F2 E1 error indicates a communication failure between the user interface (control panel) and the main electronic control board. The wire harness or ribbon cable connecting them may be loose, damaged, or one of the boards has failed.
symptoms: Buttons do not respond to presses
Dryer will not start any cycle

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes, then plug it back in and test all controls.

fixSteps.title: Reseat ribbon cable connection
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, remove the top panel or control console cover. Locate the ribbon cable connecting the UI board to the main board. Carefully disconnect and firmly reseat both ends.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check the ribbon cable for tears, burns, or bent pins. Examine both circuit boards for visible burn marks or damaged components.

fixSteps.title: Replace the user interface board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating the cable does not work, replace the UI board. Remove mounting screws, disconnect the harness, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the UI board does not resolve the issue, a technician can determine if the main control board is at fault.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer F2E1 communication error fix

faq.q: How do I know which board is faulty?
faq.a: Start with reseating the cable, then try replacing the less expensive UI board first. If that does not fix it, the main board may be the cause.

faq.q: Can vibration cause this error?
faq.a: Yes, dryer vibration over time can loosen the ribbon cable connection. Reseating the cable is a common fix.

faq.q: Can I run my dryer with F2E1?
faq.a: No, the dryer will not operate properly without communication between the control boards.

title: Exhaust Thermistor Open/Short
shortDescription: The exhaust temperature sensor has failed (open or shorted circuit).
fullDescription: The F3 E1 error code means the exhaust thermistor (temperature sensor) is reading an open or short circuit. The dryer uses this sensor to monitor the exhaust air temperature and regulate heating. Without a functioning thermistor, the dryer cannot safely control the temperature.
symptoms: Clothes remain damp after full cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute, plug it back in, and run a timed dry cycle to test.

fixSteps.title: Locate the exhaust thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and remove the rear access panel. The exhaust thermistor is typically located on the blower housing or exhaust duct near the rear of the dryer.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor wires and test with a multimeter set to resistance. At room temperature, the thermistor should read approximately 10,000-12,000 ohms. An open (infinite) or zero reading indicates failure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the single mounting screw, disconnect the wiring, and install the new thermistor. Reconnect the wiring and secure with the mounting screw.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the thermistor does not clear the error, the control board circuit may be faulty.

faq.q: What does the exhaust thermistor do?
faq.a: It monitors the temperature of the air leaving the dryer drum to help regulate heating and prevent overheating.

faq.q: Can I run the dryer without the thermistor?
faq.a: No, the dryer will not heat properly and may overheat without a working thermistor, creating a fire hazard.

faq.q: Is the thermistor the same as the thermal fuse?
faq.a: No, the thermistor is a temperature sensor that varies resistance with temperature. The thermal fuse is a one-time safety device that blows when the dryer overheats.

title: Restricted Airflow
shortDescription: The dryer has detected restricted exhaust airflow.
fullDescription: The F4 E1 error code indicates the dryer is detecting restricted exhaust airflow. The exhaust temperature is rising higher than expected because air cannot flow freely through the vent system. This is typically caused by a clogged lint filter, blocked exhaust vent, or crushed vent hose. Restricted airflow is a fire hazard and should be addressed immediately.

causes.cause: Clogged lint filter or lint trap
Blocked or clogged exhaust vent duct
Bird nest or debris in exterior vent hood
Vent run too long or with too many bends

symptoms: Dryer takes much longer to dry clothes
Excessive lint buildup around the dryer
Humid or hot air in the laundry room

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lint filter and clean off all accumulated lint. Wash the filter with warm soapy water if it has fabric softener residue buildup, then let it dry completely before reinstalling.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the vent hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dryer away from the wall and disconnect the vent hose from both the dryer and the wall. Check for kinks, crushes, or lint buildup inside the hose.

fixSteps.title: Clean the exhaust vent duct
fixSteps.instruction: Use a dryer vent cleaning brush kit to clean the entire vent duct from the dryer to the exterior vent hood. Also clean the lint buildup inside the dryer where the vent connects.

fixSteps.title: Check the exterior vent hood
fixSteps.instruction: Go outside and check the vent hood flap opens freely. Remove any debris, lint, or bird nests blocking the vent. Ensure the flap closes when the dryer is off.

whenToCallPro: For long or complex vent runs that cannot be cleaned with a standard brush kit, or if the vent duct is installed inside walls and difficult to access.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer F4E1 restricted airflow fix

faq.q: Is restricted airflow dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, restricted airflow is one of the leading causes of dryer fires. Clean your lint filter after every load and the vent duct at least once a year.

faq.q: How often should I clean my dryer vent?
faq.a: The exhaust vent duct should be cleaned at least once a year, or more frequently if you do many loads of laundry per week.

faq.q: Can I use a flexible plastic vent hose?
faq.a: No, flexible plastic or foil vent hoses are not recommended and are prohibited by some building codes. Use rigid or semi-rigid metal vent duct.

title: Communication Error Between Boards
shortDescription: The main control board and motor control board are not communicating.
fullDescription: The F6 E1 error code indicates a communication failure between the main electronic control board and the motor control board. These two boards exchange information about cycle parameters and motor speed, and this communication link has been disrupted.
symptoms: Error appears at beginning of cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes. Plug it back in and attempt to start a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel and rear panel to access both boards. Inspect the wire harness connecting the main control board to the motor control board. Reseat all connectors firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the wiring harness for chafed, burned, or broken wires. Check both boards for visible damage such as burn marks, bulging capacitors, or corroded traces.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: If connections are secure and wiring is good, the motor control board is the more common failure. Replace it and test. If the error persists, the main control board may also need replacement.

whenToCallPro: This error often requires professional diagnosis to determine which board has failed before purchasing replacement parts.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer F6E1 communication error fix

faq.q: What causes communication errors in dryers?
faq.a: Vibration, power surges, or age can loosen connections or damage circuit boards, causing communication failures.

faq.q: Should I replace both boards?
faq.a: No, start with checking connections, then replace one board at a time. The motor control board fails more often.

faq.q: Can I test the boards myself?
faq.a: Board-level testing requires specialized equipment. Visual inspection for burn marks and checking connections is the best DIY approach.

title: Power Failure
shortDescription: The dryer has detected a power interruption during a cycle.
fullDescription: The PF error code indicates the dryer experienced a power failure or interruption while a cycle was in progress. The dryer lost electrical power and is alerting you that the cycle did not complete. This is an informational code and not a hardware failure.
symptoms: PF displayed after power is restored

fixSteps.title: Clear the code
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Power or Start button to clear the PF code from the display.

fixSteps.title: Restart the cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Select the desired cycle and press Start to restart the drying cycle. Check on the clothes to estimate how much time is needed.

fixSteps.title: Check the power connection
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the power cord is firmly plugged into the outlet. For electric dryers, check that both circuit breakers for the 240V circuit are not tripped.

fixSteps.title: Verify electrical supply
fixSteps.instruction: If PF occurs repeatedly without a power outage, test the outlet voltage with a multimeter (should be 240V for electric dryers, 120V for gas). Have an electrician inspect if voltage is unstable.

whenToCallPro: If PF occurs repeatedly without a known power outage, an electrician should inspect the electrical supply and outlet.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer PF power failure code fix

faq.q: Is PF a serious error?
faq.a: No, PF simply means the dryer lost power during a cycle. It is informational and usually cleared by pressing any button.

faq.q: Will PF damage my dryer?
faq.a: A single power interruption will not damage the dryer. Frequent power surges could potentially damage electronic components over time.

faq.q: Do I need to restart the whole cycle?
faq.a: You can restart the cycle or select a shorter timed dry to finish drying the load.

title: Low Voltage / Missing Phase on 240V Line
shortDescription: The dryer is not receiving full 240V power supply.
fullDescription: The L2 error code on an Amana electric dryer indicates that one leg of the 240V power supply is missing or has significantly low voltage. Electric dryers require two 120V legs for a total of 240V. If one leg is missing, the dryer motor may run but the heating element will not operate, or the dryer may not function at all.

causes.cause: Tripped circuit breaker on one leg
Loose connection at the power outlet or dryer terminal block
Electrical supply problem from the panel

symptoms: Dryer runs but does not heat
L2 displayed on the control panel
Dryer takes excessively long to dry

fixSteps.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Go to your electrical panel and check both breakers for the dryer circuit. Even if they appear to be in the ON position, flip them fully OFF then back ON.

fixSteps.title: Test the outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter set to AC voltage, test the dryer outlet. You should read approximately 240V between the two hot terminals and 120V between each hot terminal and ground.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the power cord
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer and inspect the power cord for damage, burns, or loose prongs. Check the terminal block inside the dryer where the cord connects for loose or burned connections.

fixSteps.title: Call an electrician
fixSteps.instruction: If the outlet voltage is incorrect even after resetting breakers, an electrician should inspect the circuit from the panel to the outlet for a break in the wiring.

whenToCallPro: If resetting the breaker does not fix the issue or if the outlet voltage is not 240V, call a licensed electrician.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer L2 low voltage error fix

faq.q: Does the L2 code apply to gas dryers?
faq.a: No, L2 only applies to electric dryers that use 240V. Gas dryers run on 120V and would not show this code.

faq.q: Can a bad breaker cause L2?
faq.a: Yes, a worn or tripped breaker on one leg of the 240V circuit is a common cause. Reset or replace the breaker.

faq.q: Is it safe to use my dryer with L2?
faq.a: The dryer may run but will not heat properly. Running it without full voltage is not recommended as it could stress electronic components.

title: Restricted Airflow Warning
shortDescription: The dryer has detected restricted exhaust airflow.
fullDescription: The AF code is an airflow restriction warning. The dryer has detected that the exhaust airflow is reduced, which causes the dryer to take longer to dry clothes and increases the risk of overheating. This is a safety warning that should be addressed immediately to prevent a potential fire hazard.

causes.cause: Crushed vent hose behind the dryer

symptoms: Dryer takes multiple cycles to dry clothes
Dryer exterior is very hot to the touch
Excessive lint around the dryer area

fixSteps.title: Clean the lint filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and thoroughly clean the lint filter. If fabric softener residue is present, wash the filter with warm soapy water and let it air dry.

fixSteps.title: Check the vent hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dryer forward and inspect the vent hose for crushes or kinks. Replace flexible foil hose with rigid or semi-rigid metal duct.

fixSteps.title: Clean the full vent run
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the vent hose from the dryer and wall. Use a dryer vent brush kit or leaf blower to clear lint from the entire vent duct to the exterior.

fixSteps.title: Clean inside the dryer
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower front panel and use a vacuum to clean lint from around the blower housing, drum area, and heating element compartment.

whenToCallPro: For very long vent runs or vents that are difficult to access inside walls or on the roof.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer AF airflow restriction fix

faq.q: Is the AF code a fire hazard?
faq.a: Yes, restricted airflow is a leading cause of dryer fires. Address this issue immediately by cleaning the vent system.

faq.q: How often should I clean the dryer vent?
faq.a: Clean the full exhaust vent duct at least once a year. Clean the lint filter after every load.

faq.q: What type of vent hose should I use?
faq.a: Use rigid or semi-rigid metal vent duct. Avoid flexible plastic or thin foil hoses which can trap lint and collapse.

title: Inlet Thermistor Open/Short
shortDescription: The inlet temperature sensor has an open or short circuit.
fullDescription: The F3 E2 error indicates the inlet thermistor, which measures the temperature of air entering the drum, has failed. The dryer reads this sensor as either open circuit (infinite resistance) or short circuit (zero resistance), both of which prevent proper temperature regulation.
symptoms: Dryer does not heat or overheats
Dryer shuts down during heating phase

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute, plug it back in, and test with a timed dry cycle.

fixSteps.title: Locate the inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and remove the front lower panel or rear panel. The inlet thermistor is typically near the heater box or at the inlet to the drum.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor wires and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature it should read approximately 10,000-12,000 ohms. Replace if the reading is open or zero.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw, disconnect the old thermistor, and install the replacement. Reconnect the wiring and reassemble.

whenToCallPro: If the replacement thermistor does not clear the error, the control board may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer F3E2 inlet thermistor replacement

faq.q: Is the inlet thermistor the same part as the exhaust thermistor?
faq.a: They may use the same part number in some models, but they are located in different positions. Check your model's parts diagram.

faq.q: Can a bad thermistor cause a fire?
faq.a: A failed thermistor prevents proper temperature control, which could lead to overheating. Replace it promptly.

faq.q: How do I test a thermistor?
faq.a: Use a multimeter set to ohms. At room temperature, it should read around 10,000-12,000 ohms. Open or zero readings mean it is defective.

title: EEPROM Communication Error
shortDescription: The control board cannot read its internal memory.
fullDescription: The F1 E3 error code indicates the main control board cannot communicate with or read from its EEPROM (Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory). The EEPROM stores the dryer's configuration and cycle programming. Without access to this data, the dryer cannot operate.

causes.cause: Temporary power glitch corrupted EEPROM data
Failed EEPROM chip on control board
Power surge damaged the control board

symptoms: Dryer does not operate at all
Control panel lights up but cycle will not start

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes to allow a full capacitor discharge. Plug it back in and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Reset the circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dryer's circuit breaker for 10 minutes, then turn it back on. This provides a more complete power cycle than unplugging.

fixSteps.title: Inspect control board
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, remove the top panel, and visually inspect the main control board for any burn marks, bulging capacitors, or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace the main control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after multiple power resets, the control board must be replaced. Disconnect all harnesses, remove mounting hardware, and install the new board.

whenToCallPro: This error almost always requires control board replacement. A professional can confirm the diagnosis before you purchase the part.
videoSearchQuery: Amana dryer F1E3 EEPROM control board error

faq.q: Can I reprogram the EEPROM?
faq.a: No, the EEPROM cannot be reprogrammed by the consumer. The entire control board must be replaced.

faq.q: Will a power reset fix F1E3?
faq.a: Sometimes a power reset can clear a temporary data corruption, but if the EEPROM chip has failed, the board needs replacement.

faq.q: Is F1E3 covered under warranty?
faq.a: Check your warranty terms. If the dryer is within the manufacturer's warranty period, contact Amana for a warranty repair.
